title: pastinaca caroli-kochii
intro: Pastinaca caroli-kochii, also known as Koch's cow parsnip or wild parsnip, is a biennial plant in the carrot family that grows in Europe and western Asia. The plant's roots and leaves have been used in traditional medicine for their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. Consuming Pastinaca caroli-kochii is said to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving rheumatic pain, and supporting the digestive system. The plant can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and its leaves can also be used as a vegetable in cooking.
